top10.introHtml: Top 10 best-buy convertibles within 35 miles of Media, PA — ranked by condition, reliability, and fun factor for a cheap second car. Prices $7,000–$15,000. Refreshed 2026-05-26.

picks.whyGood: Under 38k miles on a Solstice is genuinely exceptional — these are usually driven hard. West Chester is only 9 miles from Media, perfect for a quick test drive. The Ecotec 2.0 engine is bulletproof, the top is a simple manual soft-top with minimal failure points, and the chassis delivers genuine sports-car feedback at this price.
picks.whatToVerify: Cycle the soft-top fully (open and close) — check the rear window seam and zipper condition. Inspect rocker panels and sills for rust, especially at the body-to-frame seam. Pull the carpet for water stains. Ask for maintenance records on the 2.0 Ecotec; no major issues but verify oil changes and belt service.
picks.bottomLine: 37k-mile Solstice 9 miles from home — this is the pick of the week.

picks.whyGood: The Saturn Sky shares its Kappa platform with the Solstice but has a slightly more refined interior and standard features. Under 40k miles on a Cars.com Great Deal badge at $9,995 is excellent value. J&S Autohaus III is a reputable independent dealer. Clean title and no accidents verified by Cars.com filters.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft-top through a full cycle. Check headliner and carpet for water intrusion. Inspect rocker panels and door sills. Verify the Ecotec timing chain service history. Test A/C — the condenser is exposed and often gets stone-chipped.
picks.bottomLine: Nearly new Sky at a Great Deal price — hard to beat for cheap convertible fun.

picks.whyGood: 32,350 miles on a 2006 Solstice is astonishing — likely a garage-kept second car, exactly what this search is shopping for. Clean title, no accidents, Good Deal rating. At $12,995 you're paying a small premium but getting a nearly unmolested example with 20-year upside.
picks.whatToVerify: Full soft-top cycle. Rocker panels are the classic rust spot — inspect carefully underneath. Verify rubber seals around the top frame are not cracked or shrunk. Check for any stored-car issues: tires, belts, fluid condition despite low miles.
picks.bottomLine: 32k-mile Solstice is essentially a time capsule — pay the $13k and enjoy it for years.

picks.whyGood: Mid-engine layout, 1ZZ-FE 4-cylinder Toyota reliability, and a sequential manual gearbox option make the MR2 Spyder one of the most driver-focused cars under $15k anywhere. Under 75k miles at $9,250 is a genuine find. Toyota drivetrains at this mileage are barely broken in.
picks.whatToVerify: SMT (sequential manual) gearbox — if equipped, verify actuator function and no harsh shifts. Manual top mechanism operates smoothly. Check for rear-end rust (moisture trap behind the engine). Inspect for timing chain stretch and coolant system leaks (coolant passages are mid-mounted and can weep). Verify no Walmart tune-ups.
picks.bottomLine: Mid-engine Toyota joy for under $10k — if it checks out, buy it immediately.

picks.whyGood: The E46 325Ci is widely regarded as one of BMW's best driving machines — balanced, communicative, and still reasonably affordable to maintain. 60k miles on a 2001 at $10,000 with a Great Deal badge is a legitimate find. Mancini Motors is a local independent with steady inventory.
picks.whatToVerify: BMW soft-top hydraulic rams — check both rams cycle fully without hesitation or drift. Oil leaks from valve cover gasket, oil pan gasket, and rear main seal are common at this age — inspect underneath. Coolant expansion tank and hoses (crack at 20+ years). VANOS rattle on cold start. Rear subframe mounting points for rust.
picks.bottomLine: Great Deal E46 at 60k miles — classic BMW feel for $10k.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 with the M52B25 inline-six is a proper roadster — better than the four-cylinder cars in every measurable way. 75k miles is modest, Great Deal rating, and Selden Motors is a known mid-Atlantic BMW specialist. Clean top, no accidents per Cars.com filters.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft-top latches and hydraulic assist (if power-top variant). Oil leaks from valve cover and oil filter housing are Z3 staples — look for staining on the block. Check for rust at the sill / tub junction underneath. VANOS functionality. Rear diff bushings — clunk on throttle lift.
picks.bottomLine: Inline-six Z3 roadster at a Great Deal price — quintessential cheap fun convertible.

picks.whyGood: The R230 SL500 is a V8-powered retractable hardtop — arguably the most capable car on this list. 54k miles at $11,989 with a Great Deal badge is exceptional for a car that cost $85k new. ABC hydraulic suspension is the main risk factor but this price is cheap enough to absorb it.
picks.whatToVerify: ABC (Active Body Control) suspension — check for any warning lights, especially after sitting. Retractable hardtop mechanism: run it 5 full cycles while watching for hesitation or error codes. Inspect trunk floor for water intrusion from top seals. V8 oil leaks (cam cover gaskets). Battery condition (accessory load is extreme).
picks.bottomLine: V8 retractable hardtop for $12k — massive car at a fraction of its original price.

picks.whyGood: The GXP adds a turbocharged 2.0 LNF engine — 260 hp in a 2,900-lb roadster. At $8,195 with a Great Price rating, this is the best performance-per-dollar on the list. 88k miles is perfectly manageable on the LNF with proper maintenance. Pennsauken is 18 miles away.
picks.whatToVerify: LNF turbo — check for oil consumption (common), inspect intercooler connections and boost hoses for cracks. Soft-top rear window condition (older GXPs can yellow). Rocker panel rust at seam. Clutch condition if manual. Verify no aftermarket tune that might stress the stock internals.
picks.bottomLine: Turbocharged Solstice GXP for $8,200 — the hot rod pick of this batch.

picks.whyGood: The 128i uses BMW's proven N52 inline-six — smooth, durable, and rewarding to rev. 69k miles at $9,500 with a Great Price badge is well below market. Fairless Hills is at the 35-mile edge of the search radius but worth the drive for this combination.
picks.whatToVerify: BMW power soft-top — full cycle test. N52 VANOS actuator (rattle on cold start is normal, rattling warm is not). Valve cover gasket and oil filter housing for leaks. Cooling system hoses and expansion tank. Check for any DSC or ABS fault codes. Rear seat area for water stains from top seal.
picks.bottomLine: N52-powered 128i at a Great Price — BMW refinement for under $10k.

picks.whyGood: A 2007 GXP with 51k miles at $11,495 — the turbo Solstice with the lowest mileage on the list aside from the $13k base car. Sewell NJ is 20 miles out, easy pickup. The LNF turbo delivers 260 hp in a light chassis; with a manual gearbox this is genuinely fun to drive.
picks.whatToVerify: LNF turbo health (oil consumption, boost pressure, intercooler hose condition). Soft-top rear window and frame seals. Rocker panel rust at the body seam. Clutch wear if manual. Verify no salvage or flood history despite no accidents flag.
picks.bottomLine: 51k-mile turbo Solstice GXP — the GXP pick for drivers who want punch over polish.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles in the $7k–$15k range, ranked GT-first by trim, then mileage. SN-95 (1994–2004) and S197/S550 (2005–2022) both represented.

picks.whyGood: A 1994 GT convertible with only 44,000 miles — that's a survivor. The SN-95 GT's 5.0 Windsor V8 is one of the most reliable and rebuildable engines Ford ever made. Private seller listing suggests a personal car with actual history available. Clean title, no accidents per Cars.com filters.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible frame: inspect the convertible-top-mount-to-rocker junction underneath for rust (biggest SN-95 weakness). Soft-top mechanism and rear window (may be plastic and yellowed after 30 years). Test all T-tops/roof seals. Rear axle clunk on takeoff (8.8-inch axle). Manual transmission: third-gear synchro test.
picks.bottomLine: 44k-mile GT from a private seller — the low-mileage SN-95 Mustang find of this search.

picks.whyGood: The S197 V6 makes 305 hp — more than the original GT — with much better fuel economy and parts availability. Great Deal badge at $10,499 with 64k miles. The 2013 S197 also gets independent rear suspension, which transforms the driving dynamics over the earlier solid-axle cars.
picks.whatToVerify: IRS rear-end clunk on takeoff (common on S197 IRS). T-3650 manual gearbox: slow-shift to 3rd to test synchro. Water staining on rear seats from top seal failure. Inspect soft-top frame and rear window seam. Check for prior accident repairs — run Carfax separately.
picks.bottomLine: 305-hp V6 S197 at a Great Deal price — more modern and powerful than it sounds.

picks.whyGood: Second SN-95 GT in the search results at nearly the same price with 55k miles. Two examples at this mileage level in one search suggests these are genuinely out there — good buying pressure. The 5.0 V8 GT in the last year of the SN-95 first generation.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN-95 checklist: rocker/top-mount rust, rear window condition, rear axle clunk, manual gearbox synchros. At 55k miles, check brake lines and rubber suspension bushings for age-related deterioration.
picks.bottomLine: 55k-mile SN-95 GT — solid backup if the 44k-mile private car falls through.

picks.whyGood: The S550 EcoBoost Premium is the most modern Mustang in this search — independent rear suspension, modern infotainment, better fuel economy. The 2.3T EcoBoost makes 310 hp. The catch: 141k miles is high for $13k and the turbo adds a maintenance dimension the V8 cars don't have.
picks.whatToVerify: EcoBoost turbo: inspect intercooler connections, check for oil consumption, verify no boost leaks. S550 soft-top: electronic mechanism test (both directions, check for hesitation). IRS bushings and rear differential fluid condition at this mileage. Request full service history — 141k miles needs documentation.
picks.bottomLine: Modern S550 EcoBoost but 141k miles at $13k is a lot — prioritize the lower-mileage GT picks first.
